claim: The workflow retains rejection and assumption states through explicit topology and electrical readiness gates.
purpose: Summarize pipeline stages, quality gates, and persistent diagnostic boundaries.
caption: Fail-closed reconstruction and release workflow. Blue boxes are transformations, yellow diamonds are gates, green boxes are retained artifacts, and red boxes are diagnostic or rejected outputs. The diagram is conceptual and does not encode measured performance.
provenance_notes: Authored as draw.io XML and exported without manual post-processing.
notes: No manual edits to data-bearing outputs.

claim: The selected strategy converts 5,334 raw line features into 1,342 circuits and retains 358 inter-facility candidates while preserving rejected classes.
purpose: Show the reconstruction funnel and complete selected-strategy circuit disposition.
caption: Reconstruction funnel and circuit disposition for the selected node-set B, 100 m facility-buffer, 0.5 m endpoint-snap, voltage-status-aware strategy. Counts describe pipeline outputs from the current E-REDES snapshot; retained means passing declared geometric rules, not operator validation.
uncertainty: Counts are deterministic for the snapshot but classifications depend on facility coverage and thresholds.
provenance_notes: Generated directly from the topology summary; no geographic identifiers are plotted.
notes: No manual edits to data-bearing outputs.

claim: The all-facility candidate graph remains fragmented despite a 53-facility largest component.
purpose: Report aggregate graph fragmentation without disclosing coordinates or facility identifiers.
caption: Aggregate structure of the selected candidate graph. Component sizes are shown on a logarithmic vertical scale; the degree histogram retains parallel edges. The graph contains 484 facilities and 358 branch records, including 109 isolated facilities. Exact coordinates and identifiers are omitted from the figure.
uncertainty: Graph metrics are exact for the inferred candidate, but physical correctness lacks operator ground truth.
provenance_notes: Computed from GraphML using NetworkX; only aggregate statistics are plotted.
notes: No manual edits to data-bearing outputs.

claim: Retained branch yield, ambiguity, and connected-component size vary materially with facility buffers and endpoint snapping.
purpose: Expose threshold sensitivity and the trade-off used by the internal selection heuristic.
caption: Topology-reconstruction sensitivity. Panels a-c hold node set B and voltage-status-aware merging fixed while varying facility buffer and endpoint snap; the outlined star marks the selected 100 m/0.5 m cell. Panel d shows all 216 strategies and marks the internal recommendation. Selection balances retained branches, ambiguity, and connectivity; it is not a comparison against operator ground truth.
uncertainty: Discrete parameter sweep; no statistical error bars because each configuration is deterministic.
provenance_notes: Generated from all 216 sweep rows with untruncated axes.
notes: No manual edits to data-bearing outputs.

claim: Topology fields are covered, whereas key line and transformer electrical fields require scenario or benchmark assumptions.
purpose: Separate directly available/source-backed, scenario/benchmark, and missing electrical fields.
caption: Parameter coverage and provenance in the optional electrical extension. Panel a reports row-wise status in the pre-solver candidate schema; denominators differ by object table and are normalized to percentages. Panel b reports the 358 diagnostic line rows by provenance. These categories do not imply Portuguese electrical or operator validation.
uncertainty: Status counts are exact for the tables; engineering validity of scenario and benchmark values is unresolved.
provenance_notes: Status categories are mapped conservatively; missing is not converted to a numerical placeholder in panel a.
notes: No manual edits to data-bearing outputs.

claim: The release design progresses from frozen tables to framework-neutral adapters with cross-cutting governance metadata.
purpose: Describe the Stage 1-5 contracts and validation layers without depicting experimental measurements.
caption: Stage 1-5 dataset and consumer architecture. Each stage adds an interface contract while preserving provenance, diagnostic status, split controls, and publication flags. Future framework-specific tensor bindings are explicitly outside the current release.
provenance_notes: Authored as draw.io XML and exported without manual post-processing.
notes: No manual edits to data-bearing outputs.
